task_completion.description: Verify the new folder 'ILUG-Events' was created in the email client.

task_completion.description: Verify that emails with '[ILUG]' in the subject line have been moved to the 'ILUG-Events' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the contents of the 'ILUG-Events' folder in the email client and verify it contains emails with '[ILUG]' in their subject lines.

task_completion.description: Verify the total number of moved emails is recorded in 'ilug_count.txt'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or file manager showing a file named 'ilug_count.txt' (in ~/Documents) that contains the number '11'.

task_completion.description: Verify the most recent '[ILUG]' email is selected for forwarding.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the email titled 'Re: [ILUG] eircoms adsl modems' (dated Thu, 29 Aug 2002) being opened or actively forwarded in the email client.

task_completion.description: Verify the forward recipient is correct.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the email compose/forward window and check that the 'To' field contains 'travel-approvals@company.com'.

task_completion.description: Verify the forward message body contains the required text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the email compose/forward window and check that the message body contains the text: 'Please approve trip based on this update'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical email client to organize and forward emails.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show an actual email client GUI (like Thunderbird) being used, rather than terminal commands manipulating mail files.

integrity.description: Verify the forwarding action is taking place in a real email compose window.
integrity.visual_evidence: The recipient and message body should be typed into the email client's standard compose/forward window, not mocked up in a generic text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands to bypass GUI interactions for counting or file creation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file 'ilug_count.txt' should ideally be seen in a GUI text editor or file manager, without evidence of terminal commands like 'grep' or 'echo' being used as a shortcut to complete the task.
